name: WiringDiagram

unions.name: Diagram
unions.comment:
Represent an abstract wire-diagram.

Think of boxes connected with ports, that can be inside other boxes (which connect outside)
(TODO illustration)
cases: { label : Maybe.Maybe a, wrap : Basics.Bool, inner : List.List (WiringDiagram.Diagram a), direction : WiringDiagram.Direction, inPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports, outPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports }

unions.name: Direction
unions.comment:
Direction of composition of diagrams

D1 is horizontal (sequenced, chained)
D2 is vertical (in paralell)

aliases.name: Ports
aliases.comment: Ports are a list of numbers identifying each port

values.name: diagram
values.comment: Wrap a diagram, exposing the free in and out ports
values.type: { a | label : Maybe.Maybe b, inner : List.List (WiringDiagram.Diagram b) } -> WiringDiagram.Diagram b

values.name: inParallel
values.comment:
Put diagrams in paralell. No connections will be made.
(We probably need to relabel ports here...)

values.name: inPorts
values.comment: Extract the input ports of a Diagram

values.name: inSequence
values.comment: Connect diagrams in a chain

values.name: include
values.comment:
Include a list of diagrams inside another diagram.
The included list will replace any previous innards.
values.type: List.List (WiringDiagram.Diagram a) -> WiringDiagram.Diagram a -> WiringDiagram.Diagram a

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialize an empty Diagram. The idea is a box with no label.
The box is abstract and will be given geometry using [the Layout module](@Layout)

Use modifiers like `setInPorts` to configure further

values.name: initLabeled
values.comment:
Initialize an empty Diagram with a label of a chosen type

All diagrams you intend to combine needs to have the same label type

values.name: initWrap
values.comment: Wrap a diagram, exposing the free in and out ports

values.name: map
values.comment:
Map a function over a diagram innards
You should avoid using this
values.type: ({ label : Maybe.Maybe a, wrap : Basics.Bool, inner : List.List (WiringDiagram.Diagram a), direction : WiringDiagram.Direction, inPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports, outPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports } -> { label : Maybe.Maybe b, wrap : Basics.Bool, inner : List.List (WiringDiagram.Diagram b), direction : WiringDiagram.Direction, inPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports, outPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports }) -> WiringDiagram.Diagram a -> WiringDiagram.Diagram b

values.name: offsetPorts
values.comment:
Bump the port numbers of a diagram.

This is used to avoid duplicate ports when combining diagrams in paralell
(relabel ports here...)

values.name: outPorts
values.comment: Extract the output ports of a Diagram

values.name: relation
values.comment: Make a relation with label a and a number of inPorts and outPorts

values.name: setDirection
values.comment: Say which direction the children should lay out
values.type: WiringDiagram.Direction -> { a | direction : WiringDiagram.Direction } -> { a | direction : WiringDiagram.Direction }

values.name: setInPorts
values.comment: Assign input ports to a Diagram
values.type: List.List Basics.Int -> { a | inPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports } -> { a | inPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports }

values.name: setLabel
values.comment:
Assign a label to a Diagram

    setLabel (Just "node") <| diagram --
values.type: Maybe.Maybe a -> WiringDiagram.Diagram a -> WiringDiagram.Diagram a

values.name: setOutPorts
values.comment: Assign output ports to a Diagram
values.type: List.List Basics.Int -> { a | outPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports } -> { a | outPorts : WiringDiagram.Ports }

values.name: setWrap
values.comment: Specify that a diagram exterior is significant
values.type: a -> { b | wrap : a } -> { b | wrap : a }

values.name: sink
values.comment: Make a sink with label a and a number of inPorts

values.name: source
values.comment: Make a source with label a and a number of outPorts

name: WiringDiagram.Examples
comment:
Some example diagrams

@docs sampleDiagrams, a, b, c, a2, b2, c2, twoLanes

values.name: a
values.comment:
A simple source labeled 'a'
It has one output port.

values.name: a2
values.comment:
A simple source labeled 'a2'
It has two output ports

values.name: b
values.comment:
A simple relation labeled 'b'
It has one input and one output port

values.name: b2
values.comment:
A simple relation labeled 'b2'
It has two input and output ports

values.name: c
values.comment:
A simple sink labeled 'c'
It has one input port

values.name: c2
values.comment:
A simple sink labeled 'c2'
It has two input ports

values.name: sampleDiagrams
values.comment:
A list of some diagrams

    sampleDiagrams : List Diagram
    sampleDiagrams =
        [ inSequence [ a, b, c ]
        , inSequence [ wrap a, wrap b ]
        , inSequence [ wrap a, wrap (inParallel [ b, c |> offsetPorts 1 ]) ]
        , wrap_a
        , inSequence
            [ wrap (source "a" 2)
            , sink "b" 2
            ]
        , wrap_axb_c
        , inSequence [ a, b ]
        , inSequence [ source "a3" 3, sink "b2" 2 ]
        ]

values.name: twoLanes
values.comment:
Two sequenced diagrams in parallel

    inParallel
        [ inSequence [ a, c ]
        , inSequence [ source "d" 1, sink "e" 1 ]
        ]

name: WiringDiagram.Layout

unions.name: Layout
unions.comment: The Layout type keeps information about the layout of a Wiring diagram.
cases: { transform : WiringDiagram.Layout.Transform, exterior : Maybe.Maybe (WiringDiagram.Layout.Box.Box a), interiorTransform : WiringDiagram.Layout.Transform, interior : List.List (WiringDiagram.Layout.Layout a) }
{ label : String.String, tail : WiringDiagram.Vec2.Vec2, head : WiringDiagram.Vec2.Vec2 }

unions.name: Polarity
unions.comment: Polarity of a Port. Is the port going In or Out of something

values.name: arrowExtent
values.comment: Make an extent for an Arrow. This stupidly assumes the tail is < head
values.type: { a | tail : b, head : c } -> { lo : b, hi : c }

values.name: layoutDiagram
values.comment: Layout a Diagram with default configuration

values.name: layoutDiagramWithConfig
values.comment: Layout a Diagram with custom configuration
values.type: WiringDiagram.Layout.Config.Config a -> WiringDiagram.Diagram a -> WiringDiagram.Layout.Layout a

values.name: portPositionsOfBox
values.comment: List the port positions of a Box, given a Polarity (In,Out)
values.type: WiringDiagram.Layout.Polarity -> WiringDiagram.Layout.Box.Box a -> List.List ( Basics.Int, WiringDiagram.Layout.Pos )

name: WiringDiagram.Svg

aliases.name: Viewport
aliases.comment: Placement and dimensions of a Viewport to render SVG inside
aliases.type: { width : Basics.Float, height : Basics.Float, xMin : Basics.Float, yMin : Basics.Float }

values.name: diagramToSvg
values.comment: Shortcut render a Diagram (via Layout) to Svg

values.name: largeViewport
values.comment: A larger sized viewport (1024x768)
values.type: { width : number, height : number, xMin : number, yMin : number }

values.name: layoutToSvg
values.comment: Render a Layout to Svg

values.name: layoutToSvgWithConfig
values.comment: Render a Layout to Svg with configurable labeling
values.type: WiringDiagram.Svg.Config.Config a -> WiringDiagram.Layout.Layout a -> Svg.Svg msg

values.name: mediumViewport
values.comment: A medium sized viewport (640x480)
values.type: { width : number, height : number, xMin : number, yMin : number }

values.name: simpleDiagramToSvg
values.comment: Shortcut render a Diagram with String labels (via Layout) to Svg

values.name: simpleLayoutToSvg
values.comment: Render a (Layout String) to Svg

values.name: smallViewport
values.comment: A small viewport of 200x200
values.type: { width : number, height : number, xMin : number, yMin : number }

values.name: view
values.comment: Render a list of Svg items in a viewport to Html

values.name: wideViewport
values.comment: A wide but low viewport of 1200x150
values.type: { width : number, height : number, xMin : number, yMin : number }

name: WiringDiagram.Svg.Config

unions.name: Config
unions.comment: Preliminary way to control how labels turn into String for SVG

values.name: applyToLabelString
values.comment: Apply the toLabelString function of the config to a value of type a

values.name: forStringLabels
values.comment: Simple starting config when labels are already String

values.name: initWithLabelToString
values.comment: Init needs a labelToString function

values.name: withLabelToString
values.comment: Simple starting config when labels are already String
